title: validate_cron does not handle step+range combinations like 0-30/5 (incomplete parser)
description:
## Severity
Minor

## Dimension
Functional Correctness / KISS (the parser is custom when a library exists)

## Location
src/tools/cron.rs:59-140 (validate_cron)

## Violation
The hand-rolled validator handles:
- "*" (line 82)
- "*/N" step-from-zero (lines 85-103)
- "A-B" ranges (lines 105-125)
- "A,B,C" comma lists (lines 127-138)

It does NOT handle the common combination "A-B/N" (e.g. "0-30/5 * * * *" meaning every 5 minutes from 0 to 30). It also rejects field "1,3-5,8" because the comma branch parses each piece as a flat integer (line 128: `val_str.parse::<u32>()`) — "3-5" fails to parse and is reported as "Invalid value '3-5'." Test test_validate_cron_valid only exercises the simple cases; the parser is incomplete by construction.

Worse, the validator is custom and incomplete when the cron crate (already on crates.io, MIT-licensed, ~50 LOC import) handles the full POSIX cron grammar.

## Mandated refactor
Replace the entire validate_cron function with a dependency on the cron crate: `cron::Schedule::from_str(expr).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;`. The crate validates field count, ranges, steps, ranges-with-steps, comma lists, and named months/weekdays. Delete the ~80 lines of hand-rolled parser and its incomplete test cases.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
